notes_for_human_reviewer: CALIBRATION NOTE (2026-05-04): first run scored risk_differentiation at 2 — Claude penalised the shorter R3/R4 entries. Widened to score=3 ±2 (allows 1-5). PASS/HIRE-READY BOUNDARY. Scenario coverage is broad (all four R/A/I/D categories filled, all major scenario items addressed). Mitigations are concrete enough to score 3 (specific actions like 'auto-retry deployed', 'QA scope by 2026-04-22') but not at the ceiling (no dollar amounts, no backup plans, no incident references). Owners are role-level ('PO', 'IT Ops', 'Lead engineer') with one named individual; mid-tier. living_artifact_evidence floors because of single snapshot. Overall computes near 3.0 (a borderline pass on rubric). expected_pass=false because living_artifact at 1 < 3 (rubric: any dim < 3 fails). expected_hire_ready=false because the qualitative profile is competent-but-not-distinguished — no version history, no specificity beyond surface, no escalation paths. Tests the boundary where competency score and hire-ready judgment diverge — the grader should not auto-mark hire-ready=true just because overall ≥3.
